name: Biscotti- Rosso
recipeInstructions:
Note: Use hazelnuts, almonds, or pistachios.
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F. Lightly spray or wipe a large baking sheet with vegetable oil.
2. In the bowl of an electric mixer, combine the eggs, egg whites, and vanilla. Mix until well combined. Add the sugar, flour, baking soda, and salt; beat until a dough forms. Stir in the nuts.
3. Turn the sticky dough onto a lightly floured surface. With clean, floured hands and with the aid of a spatula or pastry scraper, knead the dough several times. Divide the dough into two halves.
4. With floured hands, form the dough into two flattish logs, about 15 inches long and 2 inches wide. Place the logs on the baking sheet, 3 inches apart. Bake for 40 minutes. Remove from oven and cool for 10 minutes.
5. Reduce the heat to 275°F.
6. On a cutting board, cut the logs diagonally into 1/4 inch-thick slices. Arrange the slices on the baking sheet and bake for 10 to 12 minutes. Turn the slices and bake for another 10 to 12 minutes. Turn off the heat, and leave the biscuits in the oven for 15 minutes to crisp. Remove from oven and cool completely.
